timeline_correlation: Rollback ownership became unclear 41 minutes after queue latency spiked, then leadership requested a single customer-impact owner two days later.
why: Platform and application teams disagreed on who owned rollback settings while release management lacked a current runbook. That coordination gap delayed decision-making during the migration incident.
recommended_action: Assign a single migration incident commander and publish explicit rollback ownership before the next deployment window.

evidence.quote: Rollback owner unclear. Platform says app owns config. App says platform owns queue settings.
NOVA-2201: Document rollback ownership for migration flags remained blocked.
Rollback ownership was unclear between platform and application teams.

timeline_correlation: Customer onboarding complaints appeared after the migration cutover, then escalated into SLA review, service credit, refund, and churn indicators within 72 hours.
why: Support tickets show enterprise customers moving from technical complaints to commercial consequences. The operational issue is no longer contained inside engineering.
recommended_action: Send proactive remediation timelines to affected enterprise customers and create a shared customer-impact room with support, platform, and release leads.

evidence.quote: ArdentBank requested SLA review after repeated failed provisioning.
CedarWorks threatened churn if API instability continued this week.

timeline_correlation: Critical defects reopened after the failed rollback, then QA certification and the May release train became blocked.
why: Reopened bugs and blocked QA work indicate that the migration is now delaying planned delivery, not just creating transient incident noise.
recommended_action: Freeze non-critical releases until queue behavior is validated under production-like load and QA has a certifiable hotfix candidate.

evidence.quote: NOVA-2281: Retry patch conflicts with migration flag under load was reopened.
NOVA-2312: Resume May release train after migration stabilization remained blocked.
Release train slipped again. Three dependencies blocked and QA cannot certify.

timeline_correlation: A credential-like token was posted during incident response minutes after a failed rollback, then the governance layer blocked it before model processing.
why: Incident pressure caused sensitive operational data to appear in ordinary collaboration channels. The AI boundary needs to block this before external analysis.
recommended_action: Rotate the exposed credential, audit payment retry service access logs, and keep outbound model sanitization enforced for incident channels.

evidence.quote: Temporary key is GSK_live_91f4...21 and expires tonight.
Credential-like token removed before outbound AI processing.
Rotate that key immediately. Treat this as exposed.
